name: EGFRm-001
rationale: Pyrido[3,2-d]pyrimidin-4(3H)-one regioisomer with N3-aryl CF3 group pointing into Met790M hydrophobic pocket and C7-acetylpiperazine for solvent exposure, hinge binding via lactam C=O to Met793.
expected_advantages: CF3-ortho-phenyl fills bulky Met790M pocket selectively
Lactam carbonyl H-bonds Met793 backbone
Acetylpiperazine improves aqueous solubility
MW ~450, LogP ~3.2, within oral drug space

name: EGFRm-002
rationale: Pyrazolo[3,4-b]pyridine NH anchors to Met793 hinge while the CF3-pyrazole tail occupies Met790M gatekeeper pocket; morpholine-isoquinoline arm points toward solvent.
expected_advantages: Pyrazole NH donates H-bond to Met793
Trifluoromethyl group exploits enlarged Met790M pocket
Morpholine provides solubility without basicity issues
Novel scaffold distinct from all known resistance TKIs

name: EGFRm-003
rationale: 2-Aminopyrimidine fused with 4-pyridyl and tolyl groups positions the amine for Met793 H-bond while 4-pyridyl side chain engages Asp855 via H-bond; methylpiperazine improves bioavailability.
expected_advantages: Pyrimidine N1/N3 engage hinge region
Biphenyl-like tolylpyridyl bulk fits Met790M
Methylpiperazine reduces efflux and improves CNS penetration
Reversible binding, no reactive warhead

name: EGFRm-004
rationale: 4H-Pyrido[3,2-d][1,3]oxazin-4-one replaced by 3H-pyrrolo[2,3-d]pyrimidin-4(5H)-one hinge binder with CF3/Cl-disubstituted aniline exploiting Met790M steric expansion.
expected_advantages: Lactam NH and carbonyl dual H-bond to Met793
CF3 and Cl on aniline create selective bulk for T790M
Clean ADME profile, MW ~370
Minimal off-target kinase liability due to unique scaffold

name: EGFRm-007
rationale: Thieno[2,3-d]pyrimidine scaffold with C4-amino hinge donor, C2-morpholino solubilizer, and C6-(2-methyl-4-CF3-phenyl) group targeting Met790M gatekeeper.
expected_advantages: Thienopyrimidine C4-NH2 donates to Met793 backbone carbonyl
Morpholine at C2 provides aqueous solubility
Ortho-methyl-para-CF3 phenyl selective for bulkier Met790M
Sulfur in thiophene contributes van der Waals contacts in pocket

name: EGFRm-008
rationale: 2-Oxindole-5-carbonyl-methylpiperazine with C3-pyridyl substituent bridging to Asp855; oxindole NH H-bonds Met793 hinge while pyridyl N reaches DFG Asp855.
expected_advantages: Dual H-bond from oxindole NH and C=O to hinge Met793
Pyridine nitrogen contacts Asp855 carboxylate
Methylpiperazine amide improves solubility and oral bioavailability
Clean, synthesizable 3-step route from commercial oxindole

name: EGFRm-009
rationale: Quinazolin-4(3H)-one N3-benzyl variant (not 4-amine quinazoline) with N-CH2 linker to CF3-fluorobenzyl, exploiting Met790M pocket with flexible methylene reducing steric clash.
expected_advantages: N3-benzyl attachment avoids quinazoline-4-amine pharmacophore
CF3-fluorobenzyl fills Met790M hydrophobic region
Flexible CH2 linker reduces steric clash near gatekeeper
Acetylpiperazine boosts aqueous solubility and reduces ClogP

name: EGFRm-011
rationale: Carbazole-like pyrrolo[3,2-b]indole fused scaffold where the indole NH engages Met793, CF3-pyrazole arm fills Met790M, and pyridine amide with morpholine extends to solvent.
expected_advantages: Tricyclic scaffold provides high binding affinity via pi-stacking
Pyridine amide arm engages Asp855 and solvent region
CF3 group confers T790M selectivity via steric complementarity
Novel fused system with broad selectivity against other kinases

name: EGFRm-012
rationale: Imidazo[1,5-a]pyrazine scaffold with exocyclic amide to CF3-fluoroaniline for Met790M pocket occupancy and morpholine-decorated dihydro ring for solubility.
expected_advantages: Bicyclic imidazopyrazine gives novel hinge-binding geometry
Exocyclic amide NH and C=O form H-bonds with Met793
CF3-fluoroaniline selectively occupies enlarged Met790M pocket
Saturated ring increases sp3 fraction (Fsp3 ~0.35) for better ADME

name: EGFRm-014
rationale: 2-Aryl-3H-imidazo[4,5-b]pyridin-3-one where lactam NH H-bonds Met793, C2-CF3-fluorophenyl exploits Met790M hydrophobic expansion, and hydroxyethyl-piperazine improves oral absorption.
expected_advantages: Imidazopyridinone NH provides specific hinge interaction
CF3 on phenyl creates steric preference for T790M over WT
Hydroxyethylpiperazine raises solubility without TPSA penalty
Moderate lipophilicity LogP ~3.1 predicts good oral bioavailability
